originalTitle: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
title: Big News from Nigeria! A New Team Leader Joins the Race!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, bringing you exciting news from our wonderful continent! Today, we have a very important story from Nigeria, a big and beautiful country in Africa. It's like when you have to choose a new class monitor, but for a whole country! People choose someone to be their leader, called a President. And guess what? A very important person just joined a new team that wants to help lead Nigeria!

Who is Rabiu Kwankwaso?

So, who is this special person? His name is Rabiu Kwankwaso. Can you say that with me? Ra-bi-u Kwan-kwa-so! Good job! Imagine a very popular kid in your school, maybe someone who has been a great class monitor before, or who always helps organize fun games. That's a bit like Mr. Kwankwaso. He is a very important person in Nigeria who has helped people in many ways for a long time. People know him and listen to him. He's known for being strong and for having many good ideas to help his country. Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in Africa! It has so many different cultures and languages, like a giant rainbow of people!

What is the ADC Team?

Mr. Kwankwaso has joined a special group, like a team of friends who want to work together. This team is called the ADC. It stands for African Democratic Congress. Wow, that's a big name! But don't worry, it just means they are a group that wants to help make Nigeria even better. Think of it like a sports team. When a really good player joins a team, that team gets much stronger, right? That's what happened here! Mr. Kwankwaso joining the ADC makes their team very strong and ready to play a big game. This team, the ADC, is like a new group of friends who want to show everyone their best ideas. They want to make sure that children have good schools, that everyone has enough food, and that families live in safe and happy homes. They believe they have special plans that can make life even better for all Nigerians.

What Are Elections All About?

You might be wondering, 'What are they playing for?' Well, they are playing for the chance to lead the country in something called 'elections'. Elections are like a big competition where people get to choose their leaders. Everyone who is old enough gets to vote. They choose who they think will be the best leader to make good rules and help everyone in the country. It's a very important way for people to have a say in how their country is run. The next big election in Nigeria will be in the year 2027. That's a few years away, but teams are already getting ready! It takes a lot of planning and hard work to show people why you would be the best leader. Just like when you prepare for a big school play or a sports day, these teams are preparing to show their best! Did you know? When you grow up, you will also get to vote and help choose leaders! It's a very special responsibility and a way to help your country!

Who is President Tinubu?

Right now, Nigeria has a President named Mr. Bola Ahmed Tinubu. He is like the head teacher of the whole country. He makes big decisions every day to help the people of Nigeria. He is working hard to make sure everything runs smoothly and that everyone is taken care of. Now, the ADC, with Mr. Kwankwaso, wants to show the people that they also have good ideas to lead the country. So, they will be competing in the elections against President Tinubu's team. It's healthy for a country to have different teams with different ideas because it helps everyone think about what is truly best.

Why is This News Important for Everyone?

This news is super important because it means there will be more choices for the people of Nigeria. Imagine going to an ice cream shop and there's only one flavor. Boring, right? But if there are many yummy flavors to choose from, it's much more exciting! It's the same with leaders. When many different teams and people want to lead, they all try their best to show they have the best ideas to help the country. This helps everyone, because leaders work harder to make things better for you and your family. Having different people and teams who want to lead means more good ideas can come forward. Each team might have different plans for schools, or for hospitals, or for making sure everyone has enough food. People can then listen to all the ideas and choose the team they think will do the best job for Nigeria. It's like having many clever inventors all trying to make the best new toy – you get lots of amazing options!

Leaders Help Us All Grow

Our leaders, like the President and the people in these teams, work very hard to make sure children can go to school, that there are good doctors for when we are sick, and that our towns and cities are safe and happy places to live. They are like big helpers for the whole country. When important people like Mr. Kwankwaso join a team, it means more bright minds are thinking about how to solve problems and make life better for all the kids and families in Nigeria. It makes the competition to be President even more exciting, and that's good for everyone. It means that all the teams will try extra hard to come up with the best plans and ideas to make Nigeria strong and happy. We want our leaders to always do their very best, don't we?

Stay Tuned, Super Kids!

So, there you have it! A very important person, Rabiu Kwankwaso, has joined the ADC team, and they are getting ready for the big elections in 2027 to help lead Nigeria. It's like watching an exciting game unfold, where everyone wants to do their best for the country. We'll keep you updated on all the fun and important news from Africa right here!
summary: A very important person named Rabiu Kwankwaso has joined a new team called the ADC, making them stronger to compete in the big elections in Nigeria in 2027 and offering more choices for the country's future leader.
whyMatters: This is important because it means more strong teams will be ready to lead Nigeria, giving people more good choices for their country's future and making sure leaders work extra hard to bring the best ideas for everyone.

originalTitle: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
title: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
content: Locals say the structure, in a school complex, was in use despite lying unfinished for a number of years.

originalTitle: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
content: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

originalTitle: Intense US-Israeli attacks on Iranian city of Isfahan cause column of fire
title: News from Far Away: Big Lights and Fire in Isfahan
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! It's your friendly news reporter, Aisha, here from Africa! I'm here to tell you about some news from a place very, very far away. News is like a special story that helps us learn what is happening in our big world. Today, we are talking about a city called Isfahan. It's not in Africa, it's very far from us. It is in a country named Iran.

What Happened in Isfahan?

Something big happened in this city called Isfahan. People there saw bright lights and then a big 'column of fire.' Imagine a very tall, bright flame reaching up into the sky, like a giant red and orange candle. It means something big and strong happened there. Our news tells us that these bright lights and fire were caused by something that two other countries did. These countries are called the United States and Israel. They are also very, very far away from Africa and from Iran. It can sound a little bit scary, but remember, it happened a very long way from where we live here in Africa. Our homes and schools are safe.

A City Far Away Called Isfahan

Isfahan is a very old and beautiful city. Do you know what a city is? It's like a very big town where many, many people live and work. Think about our own big cities, but imagine one in a country called Iran. Iran is a country in a part of the world called Asia. It's very far from Africa, across many seas and lands. Many people live in Isfahan. The news said about 2.3 million people. That's a super big number! Imagine all the people in your town, and then many, many more. That's how many friends and families live there. **Did you know?** Iran is a country that has been around for a very long time! It has lots of history and amazing stories. It's known for beautiful carpets and yummy food like kebabs!

What is a 'Column of Fire'?

When we say 'column of fire,' it means the fire was very big and went high up into the sky. Imagine a tall, tall building. Now imagine a very bright, orange and red flame shooting up like that building. That's what a 'column of fire' looks like. It probably looked quite bright in the night sky. It's important to know that when we hear about these things, it can sound a bit scary. But remember, this happened very, very far away from us here in Africa. **Did you know?** The United States is a very big country far across the ocean! It has famous cities like New York. Israel is also a country, much smaller, and it is in a part of the world called the Middle East.

Is it Dangerous for Us in Africa?

No, dear friends, it is not dangerous for us here in Africa. This event happened a very, very long way from where we live. Our homes, our schools, and our families are safe. News reporters like me tell you these stories so you can learn about the world. It helps us understand that there are many different countries and many things happening all over the globe. **Did you know?** The Earth is very, very big! It takes a very long time to travel from one country to another. That's why Isfahan is so far from us. It would take many, many days to get there from here!

Why Do Reporters Tell Us This News?

We share news because it's important to know what's happening. Even if it's far away, it's part of our big, shared world. When big things happen, even far away, people talk about them and try to understand. We all hope that people everywhere can live in peace. Peace means everyone is happy and safe, and no one is fighting or sad. When things like a 'column of fire' happen because countries are not getting along, it means people are not in peace.

What Happens Next?

When things like this happen, people who are in charge of countries talk to each other. They try very hard to understand what happened and how to stop it from happening again. They try to find ways to make peace and be friends. Everyone wants to live in a world where everyone is kind to each other and no one has to worry about big lights or fire. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend, you talk about it and try to be friends again. Countries try to do the same. **Did you know?** Many people work hard every day to help countries get along and stay peaceful. These special people are sometimes called diplomats! They travel around the world to talk and help keep the peace.

Our Wish for Peace

So, remember, friends, Isfahan is a city far away. Something big happened there with bright lights and fire. But it is very far from us. We are safe and sound. We always wish for peace for everyone in the world. We hope that all people, no matter where they live, can be safe and happy. Keep learning, keep being kind, and keep dreaming of a peaceful world. That's all from your friend, Aisha News Reporter, bringing you news from our big, big world. See you next time!
summary: A city far away in Iran called Isfahan saw big lights and fire, but it is safe for us here in Africa.
whyMatters: It's important to know what happens in the big world so we can understand it and wish for peace everywhere.

originalTitle: At least 70 killed, 30 wounded in Haiti gang attack, rights group says
title: A Story from Haiti: Why Peace is Super Important!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends! Your friend Aisha here, reporting live from our beautiful continent of Africa! I hope you are all having a super day, filled with smiles and sunshine. Today, I want to share a story with you from a country far, far away. It’s a place called Haiti, and it needs a bit of our thoughts and kindness. Sometimes, things happen in the world that make us feel a little sad, but it’s always important to understand them and remember that there are always people working to make things better. Let's learn about Haiti and why being kind and peaceful is the best way for everyone to live.

What Happened Far Away in Haiti?

Imagine a beautiful place, an island surrounded by sparkling blue water. That's Haiti! But sadly, not long ago, some groups of people there, who sometimes make not-so-good choices, were fighting. These groups are sometimes called “gangs.” Because of this fighting, many people got hurt, and sadly, some people even lost their lives. It's a very sad thing when people fight instead of helping each other and living happily together. One group that helps look after people and tell their stories, called Defenseurs Plus, said that around 70 people were hurt very badly or lost their lives. That's a very big number, like having all your classmates and many, many more friends together. Another group, the government, first said about 16 people were affected. So, you see, the first group said many, many more people were hurt. It’s a sad situation, and it means a lot of families are feeling very sad right now. This is why we need to send them our warm thoughts and wishes for peace.

Who Are These "Gangs"?

You might hear the word “gangs” and wonder what that means. Well, imagine you and your friends playing together. Most times, you share your toys, you play fairly, and you help each other, right? But sometimes, a small group of people might decide they want to do things their own way, and they might not share, or they might even try to take things from others. When these groups get bigger and cause a lot of trouble, hurting people or making others scared, we call them gangs. They are groups of people who sometimes make bad choices and don't follow the rules that help everyone live together safely and happily. They often make others feel unsafe, which is the opposite of how communities should work. It’s important to remember that most people, all over the world, are kind and want to live in peace. These groups are just a small part of the big, beautiful picture of humanity. They don't represent all the good people in Haiti or anywhere else.

Where is Haiti, Anyway?

Did you know? Haiti is a country that is part of a group of islands called the Caribbean. It’s a long, long way from Africa, across the big Atlantic Ocean. If you flew in an airplane, it would take many hours to get there! Haiti is famous for its beautiful beaches, its vibrant music, and its rich history. The people of Haiti are very strong and have a wonderful culture. They love art, music, and delicious food! Just like here in Africa, people in Haiti have families, go to school, play games, and work hard. They are just like us, but they live on a beautiful island far away.

People Who Help

When sad things like this happen, there are always amazing people who step in to help. These are like superheroes, but without the capes! There are doctors and nurses who help the people who got hurt. There are special groups, like the Defenseurs Plus that I mentioned, who work to make sure everyone is counted and that people’s stories are heard. They try to find out exactly what happened and how many people need help. This helps other helpers know where to send food, medicine, and support. These groups are like the kind big brothers and sisters of the world, making sure everyone is safe and cared for. Did you know? There are people all over the world, from places like Africa, who travel to other countries to help when there is trouble. They are called humanitarian aid workers, and they bring kindness and help wherever it's needed most!

Why Peace is So Important

Imagine your classroom, but instead of playing nicely, everyone was always fighting over toys or who gets to go first. That wouldn't be very fun, would it? It would be noisy, scary, and no one would learn or play happily. That's why peace is so, so important! Peace means everyone can live safely, go to school without fear, play with their friends, and sleep soundly at night. When there is peace, people can build strong communities, grow food, learn new things, and make beautiful art and music. Fighting only causes sadness and makes things worse. It stops people from being able to learn, work, and just enjoy their lives. When people are kind to each other, share, and solve problems by talking instead of fighting, everyone benefits. Peace means we can all grow up happy and healthy, with friends and family around us. It's the most precious gift we can give each other.

What Can We Do?

Even though Haiti is far away, we can still do our part. We can start by being peaceful and kind in our own homes, schools, and communities here in Africa. When you share your toys, help a friend, or use kind words, you are spreading peace. You are showing everyone how important it is to be a good friend and a good neighbour. We can also send our good thoughts to the children and families in Haiti. Sometimes, just knowing that others are thinking of you can make a big difference. We can hope for a brighter future for them, where the fighting stops and peace returns. We can also learn about different countries and cultures, so we understand each other better. The more we learn, the kinder we can be!

Let's Spread Kindness!

My amazing friends, remember that every single one of you has the power to make the world a better place, starting right where you are. Be kind to your family, be kind to your friends, and be kind to everyone you meet. Let's send our love and wishes for peace to the people of Haiti. Together, we can hope for a world where everyone lives safely, happily, and in peace. Thank you for listening, and keep shining brightly! Your friend, Aisha, from Africa!
summary: In Haiti, a country far from Africa, some groups were fighting, which caused many people to get hurt or lose their lives, making everyone hope for peace and kindness.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us how sad things can happen when people fight, and it reminds us how important it is for everyone to be kind, share, and live in peace so that communities can be safe and happy.

originalTitle: Al Jazeera’s full interview with Marco Rubio on US war on Iran
title: Big Grown-Ups Talk About Big Problems Far Away!
content:
Hello, Future Leaders of Africa!

Hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super important news from around our wonderful world. Today, we're going to learn about some grown-up talks that happen far, far away. Don't worry, we'll make it easy to understand, just like a story! Sometimes, grown-ups who lead countries have very big and serious talks. They try to solve problems so that everyone can live in peace and be happy. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend, and you talk it out to make things better. Countries do this too, but on a much, much bigger scale!

Who is Mr. Marco Rubio?

Imagine a very important helper for the leader of a country. That's a bit like what Mr. Marco Rubio is! He is a special helper for the President of a country called the United States of America. The United States is a big country very far away from Africa, across a huge ocean. Mr. Rubio has a very important job. He helps his country talk to other countries. He travels to different places and meets with other leaders to discuss important things. His main job is to help his country be friends with other countries and to solve big problems without fighting. Did you know? People who talk to other countries to solve problems are called diplomats. They are like super problem-solvers who use words instead of anything else! They try their best to keep everyone safe and happy by talking things through.

Where is Iran?

Now, let's talk about another country called Iran. Iran is also very far away from us in Africa. It's in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has beautiful old cities, amazing stories, and wonderful people, just like our countries here in Africa. Iran is also a very big country with its own leaders and its own way of doing things. Just like you have neighbors in your village or town, countries have neighbors too. Sometimes, countries can have big disagreements, just like friends can. Did you know? Our world is made up of nearly 200 different countries! Each one is unique and has its own special history, food, music, and people. Isn't that amazing? It's like having nearly 200 different kinds of friends all over the planet!

Big Problems and Big Talks

Mr. Marco Rubio had an interview recently. An interview is like when someone asks you questions, and you give them answers. In his interview, Mr. Rubio was talking about some very, very serious problems between the United States and Iran. He said that the United States wants to achieve some 'objectives' very quickly. 'Objectives' are like big goals or things you want to make happen. When grown-ups talk about 'war objectives,' it means they are talking about trying to solve a really, really big problem in a very serious and fast way. It's like when you have a super urgent task to finish! But here's the important part: most times, grown-ups, especially good leaders, try their very best to talk and talk and talk to solve problems. They always want to find peaceful ways first. Thinking about peace is always the best way! Did you know? Many leaders around the world work very hard every day to prevent big fights between countries. They believe that talking and understanding each other is much better than any other way to solve problems. It's called diplomacy, and it's a superpower!

What Does 'Within Weeks' Mean?

When Mr. Rubio said 'within weeks,' he meant very, very quickly. Weeks are a short time, aren't they? So, he was saying that if the problems needed to be solved in that very serious way, they would try to do it fast. But again, this is grown-up talk about possibilities. It means they are thinking about how to solve problems quickly, but everyone hopes they can solve them by talking and finding common ground instead of taking very difficult paths.

Why Do Grown-Ups Talk Like This?

It's important for leaders like Mr. Rubio to think about all the ways to keep their people safe. Sometimes, they have to talk about very serious things, even things that sound a bit scary. They do this because they want to make sure their country is always protected and strong. However, it's also true that leaders all over the world, including those here in Africa, want peace. They know that when countries are peaceful and friendly, everyone can grow, learn, and be happy. They want children like you to grow up in a world where smiles are more common than frowns. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, people work together across different communities and countries to help each other. They share food, help with schools, and trade things. This shows how strong we are when we work together and are friends!

Wishing for Peace

So, when we hear about big talks like this, it's a reminder that grown-ups have very important jobs. They are trying to make sure the world is a safe place for everyone. And the best way to do that is always through talking, listening, and understanding each other. We can all hope that the leaders of the United States and Iran will keep talking and find good, peaceful ways to solve their big problems. Because peace and friendship are the most important gifts for all of us, no matter where we live in the world. Did you know? When countries are friends, they can help each other with things like building schools, finding medicines, and even exploring space! Friendship between countries makes the whole world a better place for everyone, especially for children.

What This Means for Us in Africa

Even though these talks are happening far away, it's always good for us in Africa to know about them. When there is peace in one part of the world, it can help bring peace everywhere. Just like a happy family makes the whole house feel good! So, let's keep learning, keep being kind to our friends and neighbours, and always wish for peace for everyone, everywhere. You are the future, and you can help make our world an even more peaceful and wonderful place!
summary: A big helper for the leader of the United States, Mr. Marco Rubio, talked about solving big problems with another country called Iran very quickly, but leaders always try to find peaceful ways first.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk about big problems between countries, it shows they are working hard to keep people safe and solve disagreements, and we hope they will always choose peaceful ways to make the world better for everyone.
